title: Current Studio Architecture
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Defines the verified repository architecture of TMS-OS at the Enterprise Documentation Reconciliation checkpoint.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document records the verified current architecture of the Two Marshalls Studios Operating System (TMS-OS) at the Work Session 086 Enterprise Documentation Reconciliation checkpoint.

It describes the architecture evidenced by the uploaded studio-portal repository. It does not authorize future components, replace repository evidence, or rewrite architectural history. Verified architectural changes over time belong in ARCH-EVO-001.

sections.title: Architecture Evidence Rule
paragraphs: Current architecture must be documented from repository evidence, tested behavior, approved project decisions, and controlled permanent records.

The repository is the evidence baseline for implemented source code. STATE-001 records the approved operating checkpoint. ROADMAP-001 governs approved direction. ARCH-EVO-001 preserves architectural change history.
Chat history, screenshots, ZIP copies, temporary files, and personal memory may support investigation, but they do not independently become permanent architecture truth.

sections.title: Verified Repository Baseline
items: Repository version recorded in VERSION.md: v0.28.1
Session and permanent-document governance modules: 48 JavaScript files
Structured StudioDB record modules: 33 JavaScript files
Department and functional HTML pages under pages/: 6

sections.title: Current Architecture Summary
paragraphs: TMS-OS is a browser-based studio operating system composed of a shared Studio Portal, modular StudioDB data, reusable rendering and search services, a structured permanent-document library, and a layered work-session and controlled-permanent-output governance system.

The architecture has progressed beyond the earlier Fully Modular StudioDB milestone. Fully Modular StudioDB remains a foundational data architecture, while the current repository also includes extensive session governance, permanent-document drafting, transaction analysis, approval, authorization, execution-boundary, verification, rollback, lifecycle, and state-transition modules.

sections.title: User Interface and Portal Layer
paragraphs: The Studio Portal is the user-facing command center. index.html loads the shared data, record modules, governance modules, search, and application behavior in a controlled script order.

Functional portal pages provide dedicated views for Documentation, Development, Assets, Publishing, Marketing, and Business. Shared layout, components, document rendering, and department-specific JavaScript support these views.
items: pages/documentation.html — permanent-document catalog and viewer access
js/layout.js and js/components.js — shared interface construction

sections.title: StudioDB Data Layer
paragraphs: StudioDB is the structured data foundation of the portal. Its architecture separates core configuration, documentation metadata, departments, database behavior, knowledge behavior, migration behavior, and individual record types into modular JavaScript files.
The loader combines supported modules for portal use. Database validation and record behavior are implemented separately from page presentation.
items: js/studio-data/core.js — core studio metadata and state

sections.title: Record Module Architecture
paragraphs: Each major information responsibility is separated into a dedicated record module. This prevents one oversized data file from becoming the sole maintenance point and allows shared systems to consume a unified set of structured records.
items: Governance records: decisions, standards, procedures, risks, technical debt, enhancements, status, milestones, work sessions
Knowledge records: lessons learned, research, recommendations, AI conversations, meeting notes, knowledge packages, studio chronicle
Operational records: projects, progress, notifications, studio commands, system configuration, releases, bugs
Studio function records: departments, assets, publishing, marketing, migration packages

sections.title: Rendering and Document Viewer Layer
paragraphs: Structured permanent documents are stored as JSON and rendered through shared document services rather than maintained as unrelated page-specific HTML.
The documentation page uses the catalog metadata in js/studio-data/records/documents.js to locate documentDataFile paths and open the structured records through the common viewer and template.
items: js/document-viewer.js — document loading and viewer behavior

sections.title: Shared Search Architecture
paragraphs: The shared search engine is loaded after the data and governance modules and before the main application script.
Its role is to provide cross-department discovery of supported studio knowledge while preserving the underlying governed records as the source content.
items: Search result styling — shared through css/styles.css

sections.title: Permanent Documentation Library
paragraphs: The repository contains 20 structured permanent-document JSON files under pages/documents. The library includes current-state, architecture, roadmap, documentation-control, procedure, standard, history, decision, risk, technical-debt, lesson, bug, milestone, work-session, and enhancement records.
The JSON documents are the canonical portal-rendered records. ARCH-001.html is a legacy companion and must not supersede ARCH-001.json.
items: Current control: STATE-001, ARCH-001, ROADMAP-001, DOC-STATE-001, DOC-INDEX-001
Governance: STD-001, PROC-001, PROC-002, PROC-003, PROC-004
History and audit: HIST-001, WS-HIST-001, MILE-HIST-001, DEC-LOG-001, BUG-HIST-001, LESSON-001

sections.title: Work Session Governance Layer
paragraphs: The session architecture provides persistent session context, controlled recording of objectives and outcomes, preparation for review, and a human approval gate before formal closure.
These modules create the operating context used by permanent-document writers and downstream transaction controls.
items: session-context.js — active work-session context and persistence
session-manager.js — objectives, completed work, file changes, tests, decisions, and summary records
prepare-session.js — review-readiness validation and review package preparation

sections.title: Permanent Document Writer Architecture
paragraphs: The writer framework creates reviewable document proposals and drafts. The registry validates compatible writer contracts, enforces one writer per registered document, orders writers deterministically, and assembles a draft package.
The writer registry does not itself write permanent files.
items: document-writer-framework.js — shared writer contract and behavior
document-writer-registry.js — registration, validation, ordering, and draft-package collection

sections.title: Permanent Transaction Analysis Layer
paragraphs: After accepted document drafts are available, the permanent-transaction architecture builds and analyzes a controlled transaction before any permanent state change is permitted.

The repository separates transaction creation, execution planning, dependency analysis, readiness analysis, manifest generation, integrity analysis, checksums, deterministic signature proposals, and manifest building into distinct modules.

sections.title: Controlled Output and Rollback Layer
paragraphs: The repository contains separate components for original-document capture, rollback-package generation, controlled execution, file-writing boundaries, execution verification, authorization, permanent-write execution, rollback execution, orchestration, review packaging, and final human approval.
The separation is intended to prevent draft generation or transaction analysis from silently becoming a permanent write.

sections.title: Permanent Documentation Lifecycle and State Control
paragraphs: The latest repository architecture includes coordinating, workflow, lifecycle, state-management, transition, authorization, approval, execution, recording, and validation controllers for permanent-document state changes.
These components are intentionally separated so that a proposed transition, an approved transition, an authorized transition, and an executed transition remain distinguishable.

sections.title: Script Loading and Dependency Order
paragraphs: index.html establishes a deliberate dependency order. Core StudioDB modules and record files load first, followed by loader, knowledge, and migration services. Session context and governance modules load next. Search loads after those dependencies, and js/app.js initializes the main portal behavior last.
Changes to script order are architectural changes and must be tested because downstream modules depend on earlier globals being available.

sections.title: Safety and Execution Boundary
paragraphs: Repository presence does not automatically prove that permanent writes, rollback execution, or state changes are enabled in production behavior.

PERMANENT-ARCHITECTURE-MAP.md records a disabled safety state at its documented checkpoint: execution authorization, permanent execution, permanent writes, rollback and restore execution, permanent state changes, and manifest mutation were disabled. Private-key signing and certificate trust validation were not implemented.
Until a later controlled test proves otherwise, documentation must distinguish implemented modules from authorized permanent execution.
items: Draft creation is not a permanent write.
Transaction creation is not execution authorization.
A deterministic signature proposal is not a real PKI signature.
Approval, authorization, execution, verification, and rollback are separate control responsibilities.
No permanent execution state should be inferred solely from file existence.

sections.title: External Preservation and Multi-Workstation Boundary
paragraphs: GitHub is the approved source-code and project-history preservation layer reflected in the repository workflow.

Existing governance records also describe a Google Headquarters human-readable documentation layer. The WS086 repository audit verified references to that layer but did not independently inspect or certify the external Google Drive contents.
Important project information must not depend on one workstation or an untracked local copy.

sections.title: Architecture Relationships
items: STATE-001 records the approved current operating checkpoint.
ARCH-001 records the verified current architecture.
DOC-STATE-001 records documentation health and reconciliation status.
WS-HIST-001 and MILE-HIST-001 preserve session and milestone history.
ENH-LOG-001 holds unapproved future improvements without changing current architecture.

sections.title: Architecture Change Control
paragraphs: Architecture changes must be approved as intentional project work. Bug fixes must not silently expand architecture, and unapproved enhancements must be recorded in ENH-LOG-001.
A verified architecture change should be reflected, as applicable, in source code, tests, STATE-001, ARCH-001, ARCH-EVO-001, DEC-LOG-001, WS-HIST-001, MILE-HIST-001, DOC-STATE-001, and ROADMAP-001.
Permanent-document reconciliation must preserve historical truth while correcting stale current-state descriptions.

sections.title: Current Reconciliation Status
items: Work Session: WS086 — Enterprise Documentation Reconciliation
STATE-001 reconciliation: installed and visually verified in the Studio Portal
ROADMAP-001 reconciliation: next after architecture approval
DOC-STATE-001 reconciliation: planned after roadmap reconciliation
Feature development: paused until core permanent-document reconciliation is complete

sections.title: Completion Standard
paragraphs: ARCH-001 is complete when it accurately identifies the implemented repository layers, their responsibilities, their dependency order, their safety boundaries, and their relationship to permanent records without presenting untested capabilities as active.
The document must remain readable as a technical blueprint while deferring historical sequence to ARCH-EVO-001 and current operating status to STATE-001.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: The current TMS-OS architecture is a modular browser-based studio operating system that combines the Studio Portal, StudioDB, structured permanent documents, shared rendering and search, work-session governance, permanent-document writers, transaction analysis, controlled-output boundaries, rollback preparation, and lifecycle and state-transition controls.

Its governing architectural rule is separation of responsibility: evidence, drafts, approvals, authorizations, execution, verification, rollback, current state, history, and future direction must remain distinct and traceable.

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified recovered architecture document to the shared structured Document Template Engine.

Reconciled the current architecture with the uploaded repository, including StudioDB, permanent-document rendering, session governance, transaction analysis, controlled-output, rollback, lifecycle, and state-control layers.

verification.method: Reconciled against the uploaded studio-portal repository during Work Session 086. Final verification requires Studio Portal rendering, search, console, and Git change-scope tests.
